meta.title: 21st Century Seapower and America's Global Interests
meta.description: At the end of the Cold War, the U.S. Navy's fleet numbered nearly 600 ships. Today, the fleet is less than half that size and notably short of the level that naval leadership says is needed. Greatly increasing national deficits, rising ship costs, and a dwindling industrial base suggest that the Navy will only continue to shrink.  Admiral Gary Roughead, Chief of Naval Operations and a member of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, will give a major address at Hudson Institute focusing on the current state
